---
title: "Function `swap()` function in the `permutations` package"
author: "Robin K. S. Hankin"
output: html_vignette
bibliography: permutations.bib
link-citations: true
vignette: >
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{dovs}
  %\usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
set.seed(0)
library("permutations")
options(rmarkdown.html_vignette.check_title = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knit_print.function <- function(x, ...){dput(x)}
registerS3method(
  "knit_print", "function", knit_print.function,
  envir = asNamespace("knitr")
)
```

```{r out.width='20%', out.extra='style="float:right; padding:10px"',echo=FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics(system.file("help/figures/permutations.png", package = "permutations"))
```

```{r, label=showdovs,comment=""}
swap
```

To cite the `permutations` package in publications, please use
@hankin2020.  Function `swap()` returns length-two cycles
corresponding to swapping its two arguments:

```{r showrform}
swap(3, 6)
```

If the two arguments are identical, the identity is returned:

```{r showidentity}
swap(7, 7)
```

It is vectorized:

```{r showvec}
swap(1, 1:8)
swap(1:8, 8:1)
```

Sometimes, printing in word form reveals structure:

```{r printswap}
print_word(swap(9:1, 1:9))
```


We may use `swap()` to effect substitution:

```{r}
options(perm_set = letters)
options(comma = FALSE)
allcyc(1:4) ^ swap(4, 26)  # replace "d" with "z"
```



# References
